title: Determinants
summary: Calculate the determinant of square matrices and understand its geometric and algebraic significance.
content_html:
The determinant is a scalar value computed from a square matrix. Written as det(A) or |A|, it encodes important properties of the matrix.

2×2 Determinant

det([a b]) = ad - bc
    [c d]

Example: det([1 2; 3 4]) = 1×4 − 2×3 = 4 − 6 = −2

3×3 Determinant (Cofactor Expansion)

det([a b c]) = a(ei−fh) − b(di−fg) + c(dh−eg)
    [d e f]
    [g h i]

Properties of Determinants

  • det(AB) = det(A) × det(B)
  • det(A^T) = det(A)
  • det(A⁻¹) = 1/det(A)
  • If det(A) = 0, A is singular (no inverse)
  • Swapping two rows negates the determinant
  • Multiplying a row by scalar k multiplies det by k
  • det(kA) = k^n × det(A) for an n×n matrix

Geometric Meaning

For a 2×2 matrix, |det(A)| equals the area of the parallelogram formed by the column vectors. For 3×3, it equals the volume of the parallelepiped.

exercises.title: Singular matrix
exercises.prompt: A matrix is singular (has no inverse) when:

exercises.title: det(AB)
exercises.prompt: If det(A) = 3 and det(B) = 4, what is det(AB)?

exercises.title: Row swap
exercises.prompt: What happens to det(A) when two rows are swapped?

exercises.title: det(kA)
exercises.prompt: For a 2×2 matrix A and scalar k, det(kA) equals:

exercises.title: Geometric 2×2
options: Area of the parallelogram formed by the columns
Length of the first column vector
exercises.answer: Area of the parallelogram formed by the columns

exercises.title: 2x2 formula
exercises.prompt: The determinant of [a b; c d] is:

exercises.title: det = 0 meaning
exercises.prompt: If det(A) = 0, the linear system Ax = b has:
options: Either no solution or infinitely many solutions (not unique)
exercises.answer: Either no solution or infinitely many solutions (not unique)
